# Corpus Notes (auto-generated by scripts/inspect_corpus.py)

Deterministic inspection of `edgar_corpus/` to ground chunking decisions in the data.
Re-generate with `python scripts/inspect_corpus.py` (idempotent — no timestamps).

## Files
- Total: **246** filings (10-K: 89, 10-Q: 157).
- Size (chars): p50 271 KB, p90 575 KB, max 1,501 KB, min 72 KB.

## XBRL blob vs prose (per file)
- Prose anchor found in **246/246** files (`UNITED STATES … SEC` / `FORM 10-K/Q`).
- Leading XBRL blob (chars): p50 30 KB, p90 95 KB, max 284 KB — this is what Stage 2 (cleaning) must strip.
- Prose body after the blob (chars): p50 231 KB, p90 497 KB, max 1,277 KB.

## Header field coverage
- `Company`: 246/246 (100%)
- `Ticker`: 246/246 (100%)
- `Filing Type`: 246/246 (100%)
- `Filing Date`: 246/246 (100%)
- `Report Period`: 192/246 (78%)
- `Quarter`: 192/246 (78%)
- `CIK`: 246/246 (100%)
- `Source`: 246/246 (100%)
- `URL`: 246/246 (100%)

> Implication: metadata extraction (Stage 3) must tolerate missing fields (e.g. `Report Period` / `Quarter`) and backfill from the filename.

## Section headings per form (files containing each item)
- **10-K**: Item 9 (81), Item 7 (80), Item 1A (79), Item 1B (79), Item 8 (79), Item 2 (78), Item 7A (76), Item 3 (76), Item 13 (75), Item 9B (74), Item 14 (74), Item 10 (73)
- **10-Q**: Item 4 (148), Item 3 (148), Item 2 (148), Item 1 (145), Item 6 (136), Item 1A (121), Item 5 (120)

## Text structure (the key chunking finding)
- Blank-line paragraphs are **scarce**: median **1** blank-line-delimited segments per filing — the prose is essentially one long newline-run, not paragraph text.
- Line lengths (chars): p50 69, p90 198, p99 4474 over 266,500 non-blank lines (long lines are tables/aligned rows).

## Decision — chunking (characters; RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter)
- **chunk_max_chars = 3000** (per-chunk MAX cap, not a fixed size), **chunk_overlap = 300** (~10%).
- Rationale: because blank-line paragraph structure is essentially absent, we cannot chunk on paragraphs. Instead the recursive separator hierarchy `['\n\n', '\n', '. ', ' ', '']` packs boundary-preserving pieces **up to** a 3000-char ceiling (~750 tokens at ~4 chars/token), splitting **within** detected sections (Stage 4) and never across them. The cap is a *ceiling, not a target*: short sections stay whole (one small chunk) and only long sections split — on the corpus ~98% of a filing's chunks land below the cap (avg ~2300 chars). 3000 chars is large enough to carry a table plus its surrounding narrative, small enough to rank precisely; 300-char overlap preserves cross-boundary context. These are a starting point — validated/tuned later by the retrieval eval.
